plan.annotation: Left: the adapter interface as supplied. Right: benchmark output from the Convex adapter run. The connector ties the commit signature to its measured latency and 1160-byte receipt, labeled as benchmark evidence rather than production proof.

operations.label: TypeScript contract
operations.value: interface CaseflowAdapter { stage(input: StageInput): Promise<Proposal>; commit(input: CommitInput): Promise<Receipt>; restore(versionId: string): Promise<Receipt>; }

operations.label: Convex adapter benchmark
operations.value: p50 38 milliseconds; p95 92 milliseconds; receipt 1160 bytes

operations.label: commit returns Receipt, measured
operations.value: commit(input: CommitInput): Promise<Receipt> corresponds to the measured Convex run: p50 38 milliseconds, p95 92 milliseconds, receipt 1160 bytes.

plan.pptxFallback: In PowerPoint, runtime numbers remain editable text and the code excerpt exports as a syntax-highlighted SVG with accessible source text; elements are grouped-editable per the supplied contract.
plan.takeaway: The TypeScript contract's commit path returns a Receipt, and the Convex adapter benchmark measures p50 at 38 milliseconds and p95 at 92 milliseconds for that path.
plan.title: Caseflow Adapter: Contract Meets Measured Runtime
